description: Data on key economic indicators (GDP, unemployment rate, inflation rate) for Los Angeles, Detroit, and Miami, used to inform the game's economic systems and world design. Intended audience: Game designers, world builders.
steps_to_find: Search government statistical offices (e.g., Bureau of Economic Analysis).
Consult economic databases (e.g., World Bank Open Data).
Review reports from economic research institutions.
access_difficulty: Easy. Publicly available data from government and international organizations.

document_name: Existing ESRB and PEGI Rating Guidelines
description: Official guidelines and criteria used by the ESRB and PEGI rating boards to classify video games, used to inform content creation and risk mitigation strategies. Intended audience: Legal counsel, content creators.
steps_to_find: Visit the official ESRB and PEGI websites.
Review publicly available rating guidelines and criteria.
Consult with legal experts specializing in game ratings.
access_difficulty: Easy. Publicly available on the ESRB and PEGI websites.

description: Official text of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), used to ensure compliance with data privacy laws. Intended audience: Legal counsel, data security specialists.
steps_to_find: Visit the official websites of the European Union and the California Attorney General.
Review the full text of the GDPR and CCPA regulations.
Consult with legal experts specializing in data privacy.
access_difficulty: Easy. Publicly available on government websites.

description: Data on population demographics (age, race, income) for Los Angeles, Detroit, and Miami, used to inform character design and world building. Intended audience: Game designers, world builders.
steps_to_find: Search government statistical offices (e.g., US Census Bureau).
Consult demographic databases.
Review reports from demographic research institutions.
access_difficulty: Easy. Publicly available data from government sources.

description: Official or widely recognized guidelines on ethical AI development and deployment, used to inform the project's AI ethics framework. Intended audience: AI and Procedural Generation Lead, Risk & Compliance Officer.
steps_to_find: Search for AI ethics guidelines from organizations like IEEE, ACM, and the Partnership on AI.
Review government reports and policies on AI ethics.
Consult with AI ethics experts.
access_difficulty: Medium. Requires searching for and reviewing documents from various organizations.

document_name: Existing GPU Market Data and Availability Reports
description: Reports and data on the availability, pricing, and performance of different GPU models, used to inform the GPU procurement strategy. Intended audience: Technical Director, Procurement Specialist.
steps_to_find: Consult with hardware vendors and distributors.
Review industry reports from market research firms.
Monitor online forums and communities for GPU availability and pricing information.
access_difficulty: Medium. Requires access to industry reports and vendor relationships.

description: Official or widely recognized guidelines on cultural sensitivity and representation, used to inform the project's content creation and sensitivity testing. Intended audience: Narrative Director, Content Creators, Community Liaison & Tester Coordinator.
steps_to_find: Search for cultural sensitivity guidelines from organizations like UNESCO and the UN.
Review reports and policies from cultural organizations and advocacy groups.
Consult with cultural consultants and experts.
access_difficulty: Medium. Requires searching for and reviewing documents from various organizations.

document_name: Existing National Labor Laws and Regulations
description: Official text of labor laws and regulations in the locations where the development team is based (Los Angeles, Detroit, Miami), used to ensure compliance with labor standards. Intended audience: Legal Counsel, HR Department.
steps_to_find: Visit the official websites of the relevant labor departments and government agencies.
Review the full text of the labor laws and regulations.
Consult with legal experts specializing in labor law.
access_difficulty: Easy. Publicly available on government websites.

description: Data on crime rates and types in Los Angeles, Detroit, and Miami, used to inform the game's narrative and world design. Intended audience: Narrative Director, Game Designers.
steps_to_find: Search government statistical offices (e.g., FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program).
Consult crime statistics databases.
Review reports from law enforcement agencies.
access_difficulty: Easy. Publicly available data from government sources.
